# pfsense_system_advanced_firewall (Resource)

[System advanced firewall and NAT](https://docs.netgate.com/pfsense/en/latest/config/advanced/firewall-nat.html) configuration including packet processing, VPN packet processing, advanced options, bogon networks, NAT reflection, and state timeouts. This is a **singleton** resource — only one instance can exist per pfSense installation.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "pfsense_system_advanced_firewall" "example" {
  firewall_optimization  = "normal"
  bogons_update_interval = "monthly"
  nat_reflection_mode    = "proxy"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `adaptive_end` (Number) Adaptive Firewall States end value. When reaching this number of state entries, all timeout values become zero, effectively purging all state entries immediately. Set to 0 for the pfSense default (120% of maximum states).
- `adaptive_start` (Number) Adaptive Firewall States start value. When the number of state entries exceeds this value, adaptive scaling begins. Set to 0 for the pfSense default (60% of maximum states).
- `aliases_resolve_interval` (Number) Interval in seconds for resolving hostnames in aliases. Set to 0 for the default (300 seconds).
- `apply` (Boolean) Apply change, defaults to `true`.
- `bogons_update_interval` (String) Update frequency for the bogon networks lists. Options: `monthly`, `weekly`, `daily`. Defaults to `monthly`.
- `bypass_static_routes` (Boolean) Bypass firewall rules for traffic on the same interface. Traffic that enters and leaves through the same interface is not checked by the firewall.
- `check_aliases_url_cert` (Boolean) Verify HTTPS certificates when downloading URL table alias content.
- `disable_firewall` (Boolean) Disable all packet filtering. WARNING: This converts pfSense into a routing-only platform.
- `disable_negate` (Boolean) Disable automatic negation of private network rules when the network matches an interface subnet.
- `disable_reply_to` (Boolean) Disable reply-to on WAN rules. This is useful for multi-WAN when using bridge interfaces.
- `disable_scrub` (Boolean) Disable all packet normalization (scrub). Disabling can cause problems and is not recommended.
- `disable_vpn_rules` (Boolean) Disable all auto-added VPN rules.
- `enable_binat_reflection` (Boolean) Enable NAT reflection for 1:1 NAT rules.
- `enable_nat_reflection_helper` (Boolean) Enable automatic creation of outbound NAT rules to assist with NAT reflection.
- `firewall_optimization` (String) Firewall optimization algorithm. Options: `normal`, `high-latency`, `aggressive`, `conservative`. Defaults to `normal`.
- `icmp_error_timeout` (Number) State timeout in seconds for ICMP error messages. Set to 0 for the pfSense default.
- `icmp_first_timeout` (Number) State timeout in seconds for the first ICMP packet. Set to 0 for the pfSense default.
- `max_mss` (Number) Maximum MSS value for VPN traffic (range 576-65535). Defaults to '1400'. Only applicable when max_mss_enable is true.
- `max_mss_enable` (Boolean) Enable MSS clamping on VPN traffic.
- `maximum_fragment_entries` (Number) Maximum number of packet fragments to reassemble. Set to 0 for the default (5000).
- `maximum_states` (Number) Maximum number of firewall state table entries. Set to 0 for the system default.
- `maximum_table_entries` (Number) Maximum number of table entries for systems such as aliases, sshguard, snort, etc. Set to 0 for the system default.
- `nat_reflection_mode` (String) NAT reflection mode for port forwards. Options: `disable`, `proxy`, `purenat`. Defaults to `proxy`.
- `nat_reflection_timeout` (Number) Timeout in seconds for NAT reflection connections (proxy mode only). Set to 0 for the default (2000).
- `no_apipa_block` (Boolean) Allow APIPA (Automatic Private IP Addressing) traffic (169.254.0.0/16).
- `other_first_timeout` (Number) State timeout in seconds for the first packet of protocols other than TCP/UDP/ICMP. Set to 0 for the pfSense default.
- `other_multiple_timeout` (Number) State timeout in seconds for other protocol connections with multiple packets. Set to 0 for the pfSense default.
- `other_single_timeout` (Number) State timeout in seconds for other protocol connections with only a single packet. Set to 0 for the pfSense default.
- `scrub_no_df` (Boolean) Clear the IP Do-Not-Fragment bit from IPv4 packets matching scrub rules.
- `scrub_random_id` (Boolean) Replace the IP identification field with random values to compensate for predictable values generated by many hosts.
- `tcp_closed_timeout` (Number) State timeout in seconds for TCP RST states. Set to 0 for the pfSense default.
- `tcp_closing_timeout` (Number) State timeout in seconds for TCP FIN-WAIT and CLOSE-WAIT states. Set to 0 for the pfSense default.
- `tcp_established_timeout` (Number) State timeout in seconds for established TCP connections. Set to 0 for the pfSense default.
- `tcp_fin_wait_timeout` (Number) State timeout in seconds for TCP FIN-WAIT states after both sides have exchanged FIN. Set to 0 for the pfSense default.
- `tcp_first_timeout` (Number) State timeout in seconds for the first TCP packet. Set to 0 for the pfSense default.
- `tcp_opening_timeout` (Number) State timeout in seconds for TCP connections that have not yet completed the handshake. Set to 0 for the pfSense default.
- `tcp_tsdiff_timeout` (Number) State timeout in seconds for TCP TSDIFF (timestamp difference). Set to 0 for the pfSense default.
- `tftp_proxy_interfaces` (String) Comma-separated list of interfaces where the TFTP proxy helper should operate.
- `udp_first_timeout` (Number) State timeout in seconds for the first UDP packet. Set to 0 for the pfSense default.
- `udp_multiple_timeout` (Number) State timeout in seconds for UDP connections with multiple packets. Set to 0 for the pfSense default.
- `udp_single_timeout` (Number) State timeout in seconds for UDP connections with only a single packet. Set to 0 for the pfSense default.
- `vpn_fragment_reassemble` (Boolean) Enable reassembly of fragmented VPN packets before processing.
- `vpn_scrub_no_df` (Boolean) Clear the IP Do-Not-Fragment bit from IPv4 packets passing through VPN tunnels.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import pfsense_system_advanced_firewall.example ""
```
